<div class="quiz-shell edu-math-calculus-checkpoint" data-default-total="5"></div>

<script>
  (() => {
    const DEFAULT_TOPIC = "limits";
    const BANKS = {
      limits: [limitMethodQuestion, factorCancelQuestion, oneSidedQuestion, radicalQuestion, valueVsLimitQuestion],
      derivatives: [averageRateQuestion, tangentSlopeQuestion, tangentLineStepQuestion, derivativeSignQuestion, differentiabilityQuestion],
      "derivative-rules": [firstRuleQuestion, powerRuleQuestion, chainFactorQuestion, productRuleQuestion, quotientSignQuestion],
      optimization: [candidateQuestion, signChartQuestion, endpointQuestion, secondDerivativeQuestion, objectiveQuestion],
      integrals: [riemannHeightQuestion, antiderivativeQuestion, ftcQuestion, signedAreaQuestion, integralUnitsQuestion],
      "integration-techniques": [techniqueQuestion, substitutionQuestion, substitutionBackQuestion, partsChoiceQuestion, numericalReasonQuestion],
      "calculus-applications": [applicationToolQuestion, unitsQuestion, motionQuestion, marginalQuestion, relatedRatesQuestion],
      "calculus-cumulative-review": [limitMethodQuestion, tangentSlopeQuestion, firstRuleQuestion, candidateQuestion, ftcQuestion, applicationToolQuestion],
    };

    document.querySelectorAll(".edu-math-calculus-checkpoint:not([data-ready])").forEach(init);

    function init(root) {
      const config = readConfig(root);
      const state = { config, total: config.defaultTotal, index: 0, score: 0 };
      root.dataset.ready = "true";
      renderStart(root, state);
    }

    function readConfig(root) {
      const node = root.previousElementSibling;
      let parsed = {};
      if (node?.classList?.contains("edu-math-calculus-checkpoint-config")) {
        try {
          parsed = JSON.parse(node.textContent || "{}");
        } catch {
          parsed = {};
        }
      }
      return {
        topic: parsed.topic || root.dataset.topic || DEFAULT_TOPIC,
        title: parsed.title || "Calculus checkpoint",
        defaultTotal: Math.max(1, Math.min(20, Number(parsed.defaultTotal || 5))),
      };
    }

    function renderStart(root, state) {
      root.replaceChildren();
      const input = element("input", {
        class: "quiz-count",
        type: "number",
        min: "1",
        max: "20",
        value: String(state.total),
      });
      const button = element("button", { type: "button" }, "Start checkpoint");
      button.addEventListener("click", () => {
        state.total = Math.max(1, Math.min(20, Number(input.value) || 5));
        state.index = 0;
        state.score = 0;
        renderQuestion(root, state, makeQuestion(state.config.topic));
      });
      root.append(
        element("p", { class: "quiz-topic" }, state.config.title),
        element("div", { class: "quiz-start" }, element("label", {}, element("span", {}, "Number of questions"), input), button),
      );
    }

    function renderQuestion(root, state, question) {
      root.replaceChildren();
      let answered = false;
      const progress = element(
        "div",
        { class: "quiz-progress" },
        element("span", {}, `Question ${state.index + 1} of ${state.total}`),
        element("span", {}, `Score: ${state.score}/${state.total}`),
      );
      const feedback = element("p", { class: "quiz-feedback", "aria-live": "polite" });
      const next = element("button", { type: "button", disabled: "" }, "Next question");
      const options = radioList(question.options, (selected) => {
        if (answered) return;
        answered = true;
        const correct = selected === question.answer;
        if (correct) state.score += 1;
        feedback.className = correct ? "quiz-feedback correct" : "quiz-feedback wrong";
        feedback.replaceChildren(
          correct ? "Correct." : `Not quite. The answer is ${question.answer}.`,
          " ",
          question.explanation,
        );
        options.querySelectorAll("input").forEach((input) => {
          input.disabled = true;
        });
        progress.lastElementChild.textContent = `Score: ${state.score}/${state.total}`;
        next.disabled = false;
      });
      next.addEventListener("click", () => {
        state.index += 1;
        if (state.index >= state.total) renderDone(root, state);
        else renderQuestion(root, state, makeQuestion(state.config.topic));
      });
      root.append(progress, stimulus(question.stimulus), element("p", { class: "quiz-prompt" }, question.prompt), feedback, options, next);
    }

    function renderDone(root, state) {
      root.replaceChildren();
      const restart = element("button", { type: "button" }, "Try again");
      restart.addEventListener("click", () => renderStart(root, state));
      root.append(element("h3", {}, "Checkpoint complete"), element("p", {}, `Final score: ${state.score}/${state.total}`), restart);
    }

    function makeQuestion(topic) {
      const bank = BANKS[topic] || BANKS[DEFAULT_TOPIC];
      return choice(bank)();
    }

    function limitMethodQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "What should you try first for this limit?",
        stimulus: "lim as x→2 of (x²+3x)",
        answer: "direct substitution",
        distractors: ["product rule", "integration by parts", "endpoint comparison"],
        explanation: "Continuous polynomials can be evaluated by substituting the approached value.",
      });
    }

    function factorCancelQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "After factoring, what is the limit?",
        stimulus: "lim as x→3 of (x²-9)/(x-3)",
        answer: "6",
        distractors: ["0", "3", "does not exist"],
        explanation: "Factor x²-9=(x-3)(x+3), cancel for x≠3, then substitute 3 into x+3.",
      });
    }

    function oneSidedQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Does the two-sided limit exist?",
        stimulus: "left-hand limit = 2; right-hand limit = 5",
        answer: "no",
        distractors: ["yes", "only if f(a)=2", "only if f(a)=5"],
        explanation: "A two-sided limit exists only when the one-sided limits agree.",
      });
    }

    function radicalQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "What algebra move helps first?",
        stimulus: "(sqrt(x+1)-1)/x as x→0",
        answer: "multiply by the conjugate",
        distractors: ["take a derivative", "compare endpoints", "use product rule"],
        explanation: "The conjugate removes the radical difference and reveals a canceling factor.",
      });
    }

    function valueVsLimitQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Can a limit exist when f(a) is undefined?",
        stimulus: "hole in a graph",
        answer: "yes",
        distractors: ["no", "only for lines", "only when the answer is 0"],
        explanation: "Limits depend on nearby behavior, not only the value at the point.",
      });
    }

    function averageRateQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Find the average rate of change.",
        stimulus: "f(x)=x² from x=1 to x=3",
        answer: "4",
        distractors: ["2", "6", "8"],
        explanation: "Compute (f(3)-f(1))/(3-1)=(9-1)/2=4.",
      });
    }

    function tangentSlopeQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "What is the tangent slope?",
        stimulus: "f(x)=x², x=3",
        answer: "6",
        distractors: ["3", "9", "12"],
        explanation: "f'(x)=2x, so f'(3)=6.",
      });
    }

    function tangentLineStepQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Which information do you need for a tangent line?",
        stimulus: "point-slope form",
        answer: "a point and a slope",
        distractors: ["two endpoints only", "area and width", "upper and lower bounds"],
        explanation: "Use (a,f(a)) and slope f'(a) in y-f(a)=f'(a)(x-a).",
      });
    }

    function derivativeSignQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "If f'(a)>0, what is happening locally?",
        stimulus: "derivative sign",
        answer: "the function is increasing",
        distractors: ["the function is decreasing", "area is negative", "the limit fails"],
        explanation: "A positive derivative means positive tangent slope.",
      });
    }

    function differentiabilityQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Which feature can make a derivative fail?",
        stimulus: "graph of |x| at x=0",
        answer: "a sharp corner",
        distractors: ["a horizontal tangent", "a positive slope", "a closed interval"],
        explanation: "At a corner, left and right slopes do not match.",
      });
    }

    function firstRuleQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Which rule comes first?",
        stimulus: "d/dx [(x²+1)⁵]",
        answer: "chain rule",
        distractors: ["product rule", "quotient rule", "constant rule"],
        explanation: "The outside fifth power is applied to the inside x²+1.",
      });
    }

    function powerRuleQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Differentiate using the power rule.",
        stimulus: "x⁶",
        answer: "6x⁵",
        distractors: ["x⁵", "5x⁶", "6x⁶"],
        explanation: "Bring the exponent down and subtract 1 from the exponent.",
      });
    }

    function chainFactorQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "What factor is easy to forget?",
        stimulus: "d/dx sin(x²)",
        answer: "2x",
        distractors: ["sin(x)", "x²", "1/2"],
        explanation: "The chain rule multiplies by the derivative of the inside x².",
      });
    }

    function productRuleQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Which structure triggers product rule?",
        stimulus: "(x²+1)(x-4)",
        answer: "two changing factors multiplied",
        distractors: ["one expression divided by another", "a constant only", "a definite integral"],
        explanation: "Both factors depend on x, so differentiate both using f'g+fg'.",
      });
    }

    function quotientSignQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "What denominator appears in the quotient rule?",
        stimulus: "d/dx [f(x)/g(x)]",
        answer: "g(x)²",
        distractors: ["f(x)²", "f'(x)g'(x)", "g'(x)²"],
        explanation: "The denominator is the square of the original denominator.",
      });
    }

    function candidateQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Which candidate type must be checked on [a,b]?",
        stimulus: "absolute max/min on a closed interval",
        answer: "critical points and endpoints",
        distractors: ["only critical points", "only endpoints", "only x-intercepts"],
        explanation: "Absolute extrema can occur inside or at the boundary.",
      });
    }

    function signChartQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Classify the critical point.",
        stimulus: "f' changes from + to -",
        answer: "local maximum",
        distractors: ["local minimum", "no extremum", "vertical asymptote"],
        explanation: "The function increases, then decreases.",
      });
    }

    function endpointQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Why check endpoints?",
        stimulus: "closed interval optimization",
        answer: "the absolute best value may occur there",
        distractors: ["derivatives never work", "endpoints are always maxima", "to remove units"],
        explanation: "The derivative only finds interior candidates.",
      });
    }

    function secondDerivativeQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "What does f''(c)>0 suggest at a critical point?",
        stimulus: "second derivative test",
        answer: "local minimum",
        distractors: ["local maximum", "no domain", "left-hand limit only"],
        explanation: "Positive second derivative means concave up.",
      });
    }

    function objectiveQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "In a word problem, what is the objective?",
        stimulus: "maximize area with fixed perimeter",
        answer: "the quantity to maximize or minimize",
        distractors: ["the derivative only", "the endpoint only", "the unit label only"],
        explanation: "Optimization begins by naming the target quantity.",
      });
    }

    function riemannHeightQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "For midpoint rectangles, where is the height measured?",
        stimulus: "Riemann sum",
        answer: "at the midpoint of each subinterval",
        distractors: ["only at the left endpoint", "only at x=0", "at the derivative"],
        explanation: "Midpoint sums use f(midpoint) as each rectangle height.",
      });
    }

    function antiderivativeQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Find an antiderivative.",
        stimulus: "∫ 4x³ dx",
        answer: "x⁴ + C",
        distractors: ["12x²", "4x⁴ + C", "x³ + C"],
        explanation: "Reverse the power rule: x⁴ differentiates to 4x³.",
      });
    }

    function ftcQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Evaluate the definite integral.",
        stimulus: "∫₀³ 2x dx",
        answer: "9",
        distractors: ["3", "6", "18"],
        explanation: "An antiderivative is x², so compute 3²-0²=9.",
      });
    }

    function signedAreaQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Area below the x-axis counts how in a signed integral?",
        stimulus: "signed area",
        answer: "negative",
        distractors: ["positive", "ignored", "always zero"],
        explanation: "Definite integrals add signed area, not just geometric area.",
      });
    }

    function integralUnitsQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "What are the units after integrating liters/minute over minutes?",
        stimulus: "rate accumulation",
        answer: "liters",
        distractors: ["liters per minute", "minutes per liter", "liters squared"],
        explanation: "Rate units times input units give accumulated units.",
      });
    }

    function techniqueQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Which technique best fits?",
        stimulus: "∫ 2x cos(x²) dx",
        answer: "substitution",
        distractors: ["integration by parts", "endpoint comparison", "quotient rule"],
        explanation: "The inside x² has derivative 2x, which is present.",
      });
    }

    function substitutionQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Choose u.",
        stimulus: "∫ 3x² sin(x³) dx",
        answer: "u = x³",
        distractors: ["u = sin(x)", "u = 3x", "u = dx"],
        explanation: "The derivative of x³ is 3x² dx, which appears.",
      });
    }

    function substitutionBackQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "What must you do after integrating in u for an indefinite integral?",
        stimulus: "substitution workflow",
        answer: "substitute back to x and add C",
        distractors: ["drop the constant", "differentiate the bounds", "compare endpoints only"],
        explanation: "The final antiderivative should be in the original variable.",
      });
    }

    function partsChoiceQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "For ∫ x eˣ dx, what is a good u?",
        stimulus: "integration by parts",
        answer: "u = x",
        distractors: ["u = eˣ dx", "u = 0", "u = x eˣ"],
        explanation: "x becomes simpler when differentiated; eˣ is easy to integrate.",
      });
    }

    function numericalReasonQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "When is numerical integration reasonable?",
        stimulus: "data or hard antiderivative",
        answer: "when an exact formula is hard or unnecessary",
        distractors: ["only for constants", "never in applications", "only before learning derivatives"],
        explanation: "Approximation is a useful tool when exact symbolic work is not the goal.",
      });
    }

    function applicationToolQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Which tool finds marginal cost?",
        stimulus: "marginal means local change",
        answer: "derivative",
        distractors: ["integral", "Riemann sum", "endpoint only"],
        explanation: "Marginal quantities are instantaneous rates of change.",
      });
    }

    function unitsQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "If position is meters and time is seconds, velocity units are...",
        stimulus: "s'(t)",
        answer: "meters per second",
        distractors: ["meters squared", "seconds per meter", "meters"],
        explanation: "Derivative units are output units divided by input units.",
      });
    }

    function motionQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "What is ∫ v(t) dt over a time interval?",
        stimulus: "velocity accumulation",
        answer: "displacement",
        distractors: ["acceleration", "instantaneous velocity", "slope of position only"],
        explanation: "Integrating velocity accumulates signed change in position.",
      });
    }

    function marginalQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "What does C'(100) approximate?",
        stimulus: "cost function C(x)",
        answer: "cost of one more item near 100 items",
        distractors: ["total cost of 100 items", "average cost exactly", "area under cost"],
        explanation: "A derivative gives local change per additional item.",
      });
    }

    function relatedRatesQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "In related rates, when should you substitute numbers?",
        stimulus: "differentiate an equation involving time",
        answer: "after differentiating",
        distractors: ["before differentiating", "never", "only after integrating"],
        explanation: "Substituting too early can erase variables whose rates are needed.",
      });
    }

    function question({ prompt, stimulus, answer, distractors, explanation }) {
      return { prompt, stimulus, answer, options: shuffle([answer, ...distractors]), explanation };
    }

    function radioList(options, onSelect) {
      const name = `calculus-checkpoint-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)}`;
      const container = element("div", { class: "quiz-options" });
      options.forEach((option) => {
        const input = element("input", { type: "radio", name, value: option });
        input.addEventListener("change", () => onSelect(option));
        container.append(element("label", {}, input, element("span", {}, option)));
      });
      return container;
    }

    function stimulus(text) {
      return element("pre", { class: "quiz-stimulus" }, text);
    }

    function element(tag, attrs = {}, ...children) {
      const node = document.createElement(tag);
      Object.entries(attrs).forEach(([key, value]) => node.setAttribute(key, value));
      node.append(...children);
      return node;
    }

    function choice(values) {
      return values[Math.floor(Math.random() * values.length)];
    }

    function shuffle(values) {
      const copy = values.slice();
      for (let index = copy.length - 1; index > 0; index -= 1) {
        const other = Math.floor(Math.random() * (index + 1));
        [copy[index], copy[other]] = [copy[other], copy[index]];
      }
      return copy;
    }
  })();
</script>
